name: Buttermilk Pancakes (L.a. Times)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, salt, and baking powder.
2. In a separate bowl, beat the egg and mix in 1 cup of buttermilk.
3. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture until the batter is smooth with no lumps.
4. Stir in the melted butter.
5. If desired, add up to 1/4 cup more buttermilk for thinner pancakes.
6. Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat and lightly grease with butter.
7. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter onto the hot surface for each pancake.
8. Cook until bubbles form on the surface and the edges look dry, about 2-3 minutes.
9. Flip the pancakes and cook the other side until golden brown, about 1 minute more.
10. Serve with syrup as desired.
Each pancake contains approximately:
60 calories; 82 mg sodium; 20 mg cholesterol; 2 grams fat; 8 grams carbohydrates; 2 grams protein.
